channel.title: NPR Topics: National
channel.description: NPR coverage of national news, U.S. politics, elections, business, arts, culture, health and science, and technology. Subscribe to the NPR Nation RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: U.S. strike on an alleged drug boat kills 1, leaves 2 survivors
item.description: The U.S. military attacked a boat accused of smuggling drugs in the eastern Pacific Ocean on Tuesday, killing one man and leaving two survivors. This brings the number of people who have been killed in boat strikes to at least 208.
item.content:encoded:
The U.S. military attacked a boat accused of smuggling drugs in the eastern Pacific Ocean on Tuesday, killing one man and leaving two survivors. This brings the number of people who have been killed in boat strikes to at least 208.

(Image credit: Jacquelyn Martin)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Haitian immigrants ask Supreme Court to toss case in light of new evidence
item.description: The lawyers argue that the court does not have a full record of how the Trump administration decided to end temporary protective status for Haitians in the U.S.
item.content:encoded:
The lawyers argue that the court does not have a full record of how the Trump administration decided to end temporary protective status for Haitians in the U.S.

(Image credit: Alex Wroblewski)
item.dc:creator: Nina Totenberg

item.title: Volunteer tinkerers bring broken appliances back to life for people in need
item.description: Sometimes a broken appliance gets thrown out even though it just needs a little fix. That's where volunteer tinkerers come in. They make it work again and give it to people in need.
item.content:encoded: Sometimes a broken appliance gets thrown out even though it just needs a little fix. That's where volunteer tinkerers come in. They make it work again and give it to people in need.
item.dc:creator: Tana Weingartner

item.title: From the middle of the ocean, Kelsey Pfendler on rowing solo across the pacific
item.description: NPR's Ailsa Chang talks with Kelsey Pfendler, who is rowing solo from California to Hawaii. She is attempting to become the first American woman to do so.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Ailsa Chang talks with Kelsey Pfendler, who is rowing solo from California to Hawaii. She is attempting to become the first American woman to do so.
item.dc:creator: Gurjit Kaur

item.title: The daily toll of no running water in San Juan, Puerto Rico
item.description: A resident of San Juan, Puerto Rico, shares the toll of living without running water for nearly two months.
item.content:encoded: A resident of San Juan, Puerto Rico, shares the toll of living without running water for nearly two months.
item.dc:creator: Vincent Acovino

item.title: On a flight home, a stranger helped her understand what came next
item.description: Rebecca Simonitsch had just learned she might be a candidate for brain surgery. The man seated beside her on the flight home pulled out a notebook to explain what lay ahead.
item.content:encoded: Rebecca Simonitsch had just learned she might be a candidate for brain surgery. The man seated beside her on the flight home pulled out a notebook to explain what lay ahead.

item.title: Iraqi soccer fans celebrate end of 40-year World Cup drought
item.description: The Iraq national men's team hasn't played a World Cup in 40 years; a drought that ends Tuesday night, to the excitement of soccer fans in Dearborn, Michigan, home to a large Iraqi diaspora.
item.content:encoded:
The Iraq national men's team hasn't played a World Cup in 40 years; a drought that ends Tuesday night, to the excitement of soccer fans in Dearborn, Michigan, home to a large Iraqi diaspora.

(Image credit: Nick Hagen for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Jasmine Garsd

item.title: Trump further guts Education Dept. by shifting oversight of special ed, civil rights
item.description: The moves to the federal departments of Health and Human Services and Justice, respectively, would further dismantle an agency that President Donald Trump has vowed to close.
item.content:encoded:
The moves to the federal departments of Health and Human Services and Justice, respectively, would further dismantle an agency that President Donald Trump has vowed to close.

(Image credit: Al Drago)
item.dc:creator: Jonaki Mehta

item.title: The war with Iran is making oil changes pricier. And a deal won't solve it
item.description: The U.S. may be the world's biggest producer of crude oil, but that's not the case for motor oil. The cost of lubricants is soaring, and even a tentative deal to end the war won't solve the problem.
item.content:encoded:
The U.S. may be the world's biggest producer of crude oil, but that's not the case for motor oil. The cost of lubricants is soaring, and even a tentative deal to end the war won't solve the problem.

(Image credit: Sergio Flores)
item.dc:creator: Camila Domonoske

item.title: Survey confirms the struggle of working parents: 'No way to be two things at once'
item.description: A new Pew survey finds many working parents feel they cannot give 100% at either work or home. Benefits like paid sick leave and more affordable childcare could help.
item.content:encoded:
A new Pew survey finds many working parents feel they cannot give 100% at either work or home. Benefits like paid sick leave and more affordable childcare could help.

(Image credit: Cliff Jette for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Andrea Hsu
